abstract:
Large language models (LLMs) pretrained on massive source code repositories have significantly advanced the field of code intelligence. However, current models face two primary obstacles. First, they typically rely on rigid architectures—either encoder-only, decoder-only, or a fixed encoder-decoder structure—which prevents them from adapting to the optimal configuration for a specific downstream task. Second, they often utilize a narrow set of pretraining objectives that may not align with the requirements of diverse tasks, leading to suboptimal performance.

To overcome these challenges, we introduce CodeT5+, a family of encoder-decoder LLMs for code designed for modular flexibility. CodeT5+ allows for the dynamic combination of component modules to suit a wide array of code-related applications. This versatility is supported by a diverse mixture of pretraining objectives, including span denoising, contrastive learning, text-code matching, and causal language modeling, trained on both unimodal and bimodal multilingual corpora. Furthermore, we implement a compute-efficient scaling strategy by initializing CodeT5+ with frozen, off-the-shelf LLMs, avoiding the need for training from scratch, and explore instruction-tuning to improve alignment with natural language prompts.

We evaluated CodeT5+ across more than 20 code-related benchmarks in zero-shot, finetuning, and instruction-tuning settings. Our models achieve state-of-the-art (SoTA) results on numerous tasks. Notably, our instruction-tuned CodeT5+ 16B sets a new benchmark on the HumanEval code generation task with 35.0% pass@1 and 54.5% pass@10, outperforming other open-source code LLMs and even surpassing OpenAI's code-cushman-001.
